risks: Public network access enabled on Cosmos DB, Redis, and ACR — production e-commerce workloads should use private endpoints to reduce attack surface
Single-region deployment with no multi-region disaster recovery — a regional outage would take down the entire e-commerce backend
Private endpoints subnet (snet-privateendpoints-001) is defined but no private endpoint resources are included in the plan
No explicit RBAC role assignment resources (acrPull on ACR, Key Vault Secrets User on KV) — these are mentioned in reasoning but missing as plan resources
No Microsoft Defender for Containers integration — WAF guidance recommends runtime threat detection for production AKS clusters
30-day Log Analytics retention may be insufficient for PCI-DSS or other e-commerce compliance requirements
AKS API server is public — payment service workloads handling sensitive financial data should use a private cluster or API server authorized IP ranges
correctionsRecommended: Add private endpoint resources for Cosmos DB, Redis, Key Vault, and ACR targeting snet-privateendpoints-001; disable publicNetworkAccess on each service after private endpoints are configured
Add explicit Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments resources: acrPull role on ACR and Key Vault Secrets User role on Key Vault for the AKS managed identity
Enable Microsoft Defender for Containers on the AKS cluster for runtime threat detection and vulnerability scanning
Consider configuring a private AKS cluster or API server authorized IP ranges to restrict control plane access
Add a resource tagging strategy (environment, service, cost-center, owner) across all resources for governance and cost tracking
Evaluate increasing Log Analytics workspace retention from 30 to 90 days for compliance with e-commerce payment processing standards
Consider adding Azure Front Door or Application Gateway with WAF capability in front of Istio ingress gateway for L7 DDoS protection and web application firewall
